title: Backport env-var-leak fix from verify_zombie_edges to cleanup_stale_edges
analysis: cleanup_stale_edges.py:run_cleanup() has a correctness bug at lines 117-123: os.environ['CONFIG_PATH'] is mutated at line 119, then FusedMemoryConfig(), MemoryService(), and CleanupManager() are all constructed at lines 121-123 BEFORE the try block. If any constructor raises, execution never enters the try/finally, so (1) CONFIG_PATH is never restored (env var leak) and (2) if MemoryService succeeded but CleanupManager failed, service.close() is never called (resource leak). verify_zombie_edges.py:run_verify_zombie_edges() (lines 152-179) already has the correct pattern: service=None sentinel before try, all env var mutation and construction inside try, guarded 'if service is not None: await service.close()' in finally. The fix is a straightforward structural backport. Existing tests in test_cleanup_stale_edges.py (TestRunCleanup class, lines 283-391) mock at the module level and test the happy path and cleanup-raises-error path, but do NOT test constructor failures — so the env var leak was never caught. test_verify_zombie_edges.py has TestRunVerifyZombieEdgesEnvVarRestore (lines 478-537) with the exact test pattern to mirror.

steps.description: Write failing test class TestRunCleanupEnvVarRestore with test_restores_env_var_when_config_constructor_fails: save original CONFIG_PATH, patch FusedMemoryConfig to raise RuntimeError('config error'), call run_cleanup(uuids=['test-uuid'], config_path='test.yaml') expecting RuntimeError, assert os.environ.get('CONFIG_PATH') == original. Wrap assertion in try/finally safety net to restore CONFIG_PATH if test itself fails. Mirror exact pattern from TestRunVerifyZombieEdgesEnvVarRestore.test_restores_env_var_when_config_constructor_fails (test_verify_zombie_edges.py lines 482-508). This WILL FAIL because env var mutation at line 119 is outside the try block.

Add test_restores_env_var_when_service_constructor_fails to TestRunCleanupEnvVarRestore: patch FusedMemoryConfig (success) and MemoryService (raises RuntimeError('service error')), call run_cleanup(config_path='test.yaml') expecting RuntimeError, assert CONFIG_PATH restored. Mirror pattern from test_verify_zombie_edges.py lines 510-537. This WILL FAIL for the same structural reason as step-1.

Write failing test class TestRunCleanupServiceLifecycle with test_closes_service_when_cleanup_manager_constructor_fails: patch FusedMemoryConfig (success), MemoryService (success, returns mock_service with AsyncMock close), CleanupManager (raises RuntimeError). Call run_cleanup() expecting RuntimeError. Assert mock_service.close.assert_awaited_once(). This WILL FAIL because in current code CleanupManager() at line 123 raises before the try block, so service.close() in finally never executes.

Restructure run_cleanup() in cleanup_stale_edges.py to match the verify_zombie_edges pattern: (1) Add 'service = None' before the try block, (2) Move 'if config_path is not None: os.environ["CONFIG_PATH"] = config_path' inside the try block, (3) Move FusedMemoryConfig(), MemoryService(), CleanupManager() inside the try block, (4) Change finally to: 'if service is not None: await service.close()'. All three failing tests from steps 1-3 should now pass, and all existing TestRunCleanup tests remain green since they mock constructors at module level.

Run the full test_cleanup_stale_edges.py test suite to verify no regressions: all existing TestRunCleanup tests (test_loads_config_and_runs_cleanup, test_closes_service_on_success, test_closes_service_on_error, test_passes_dry_run_flag), all TestCleanupManager tests, all TestQueryEdgesByTimeRange and TestBulkRemoveEdges tests, plus all new TestRunCleanupEnvVarRestore and TestRunCleanupServiceLifecycle tests pass.

design_decisions.decision: Exact structural mirror of verify_zombie_edges pattern rather than an alternative fix
design_decisions.rationale: The task explicitly asks to 'backport the same fix'. Using the identical pattern (service=None sentinel, all construction inside try, guarded close in finally) ensures consistency across maintenance entrypoints and makes the codebase easier to audit.

design_decisions.decision: Do NOT wrap service.close() in try/except for this task
design_decisions.rationale: verify_zombie_edges.py doesn't wrap close() in try/except either. Adding it only in cleanup_stale_edges would create an inconsistency between the two entrypoints. If this defensive pattern is adopted, it should be done in both files together as a separate task. The task description says 'optionally' for this.

design_decisions.decision: Write env-var tests with try/finally safety cleanup in the test itself
design_decisions.rationale: The verify_zombie_edges tests wrap their CONFIG_PATH assertions in try/finally to ensure the env var is restored even if the test assertion fails. This prevents test pollution across the test session. We mirror the same defensive pattern.

design_decisions.decision: Separate test class for service lifecycle (TestRunCleanupServiceLifecycle) vs env var (TestRunCleanupEnvVarRestore)
design_decisions.rationale: The env var tests and service lifecycle test exercise different aspects of the same structural fix. Separate classes make the test intent clear and mirror the naming convention in test_verify_zombie_edges.py where env var tests get their own class.

reuse.how: Mirror the two test methods (test_restores_env_var_when_config_constructor_fails, test_restores_env_var_when_service_constructor_fails) adapting import paths and function names from verify_zombie_edges to cleanup_stale_edges. Patch paths change from 'fused_memory.maintenance.verify_zombie_edges.*' to 'fused_memory.maintenance.cleanup_stale_edges.*'.

Backport the service=None sentinel, try-wrapped construction, and guarded 'if service is not None: await service.close()' pattern directly into run_cleanup(). The env-var save/restore logic in the finally block is already identical between the two files.

reuse.what: Existing TestRunCleanup mock and patch patterns
reuse.how: Follow the same patch target paths (fused_memory.maintenance.cleanup_stale_edges.FusedMemoryConfig, .MemoryService, .CleanupManager) and AsyncMock setup patterns used by existing entrypoint tests.
